title: Surface Areas of Pyramids and Cones

summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the lateral area formula for a regular pyramid?

Lateral Area of a Regular Pyramid L = ½Pℓ where P is the perimeter of the base and ℓ is the slant height. Surface Area of a Regular Pyramid S = ½Pℓ + B Lateral Area of a Cone L = πrℓ Surface Area of a Cone S = πrℓ + πr² Note The slant height ℓ, height h, and radius r are related by: ℓ² = h² + r².

A cone has a radius of 5 cm and a slant height of 13 cm. What is its lateral area? (Use π ≈ 3.14)

Surface Area of a Regular Pyramid S = ½Pℓ + B Lateral Area of a Cone L = πrℓ Surface Area of a Cone S = πrℓ + πr² Note The slant height ℓ, height h, and radius r are related by: ℓ² = h² + r². Lateral Area of a Regular Pyramid L = ½Pℓ where P is the perimeter of the base and ℓ is the slant height.

What is the surface area formula for a cone?

Surface Area of a Regular Pyramid S = ½Pℓ + B Lateral Area of a Cone L = πrℓ Surface Area of a Cone S = πrℓ + πr² Note The slant height ℓ, height h, and radius r are related by: ℓ² = h² + r². The other options — 'S = πrℓ', 'S = 2πrℓ + πr²', and 'S = πr²ℓ' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

A cone has a height of 12 cm and a radius of 5 cm. What is its slant height?

Surface Area of a Regular Pyramid S = ½Pℓ + B Lateral Area of a Cone L = πrℓ Surface Area of a Cone S = πrℓ + πr² Note The slant height ℓ, height h, and radius r are related by: ℓ² = h² + r². Lateral Area of a Regular Pyramid L = ½Pℓ where P is the perimeter of the base and ℓ is the slant height.

A square pyramid has a base edge of 10 cm and a slant height of 12 cm. What is its surface area?

Lateral Area of a Regular Pyramid L = ½Pℓ where P is the perimeter of the base and ℓ is the slant height. Surface Area of a Regular Pyramid S = ½Pℓ + B Lateral Area of a Cone L = πrℓ Surface Area of a Cone S = πrℓ + πr² Note The slant height ℓ, height h, and radius r are related by: ℓ² = h² + r².
